flags.concern: Step 3 changes the context's ideal editor height, but the rendered editor pane is further reduced in `EditorPaneTab`, so this plan will not reliably produce a 70% editor when the generations pane is also visible.
flags.evidence:
Revised all three steps to address the two significant flags:

**Flag step_3 (double-subtraction):** Completely reworked Step 3. Instead of just changing the ideal from 90% to 70% in PanesContext (which wouldn't survive the double-subtraction), the plan now: (a) makes `getEffectivePaneHeights` visibility-aware by only compressing when both panes are actually visible, (b) removes the secondary `generationsOffset` subtraction in `EditorPaneTab.tsx:41-43`, and (c) uses 70% ideal only when `locks.gens` is true (not hover-open, per user answer Q3). Includes a full height-trace verification for common viewport sizes.

**Flag step_2 (hydration race):** Replaced the `[location.pathname]`-only effect with the proven pattern from `useGenerationsPaneController.ts:234-238` — the effect now depends on both `location.pathname` AND `isEditorPaneLocked`, ensuring it re-fires when the persisted lock hydrates after mount. Uses `setIsEditorPaneLocked` directly (stable useMemo ref) instead of `pane.toggleLock` (unstable identity).

**Step 1 (content shift):** Added explicit test update instructions per user note — the test at line 110 asserting `paddingTop: '360px'` must change to `'0px'`, and unused mock state should be cleaned up.

**Step 4 (tests):** Expanded with specific assertions for each affected test, including which values change and why.

flags.concern: Step 2's proposed effect is keyed only to `location.pathname`, so it can miss the exact case where the persisted editor lock hydrates after mount while the user is already on `/tools/video-editor`.

flags.concern: Step 1 says to just verify `LayoutMainContent.test.tsx` still passes, but the repository already has a test asserting the current shifting behavior, so the plan is missing an explicit test update.
flags.evidence: `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.test.tsx:107-113` currently expects `paddingTop: '360px'` when the editor pane is locked. Removing the padding in `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.tsx:40` will fail that test until the assertion is rewritten.

flags.concern: Step 2 still has no targeted regression test for the route-unlock behavior, so the most subtle part of the change remains unverified by the plan's validation set.
flags.evidence: The repository has no `EditorPaneTab` test file, and Step 5 only runs `LayoutMainContent.test.tsx`, `PanesContext.test.tsx`, `usePaneLockPolicyState.test.ts`, and `useBottomOffset.test.ts`. None of those exercise `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx` on `/tools/video-editor` with a persisted `editor` lock, so the hydration-safe unlock effect introduced in Step 2 would not get direct coverage.
